sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.34 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: Let$v$be a group word, and let${ K}_v$be the class of groups$G$such that there exists a positive integer$n=n(G)$such that each element of the verbal subgroup$vG$can be represented as a product of$n$values of the word$v$on the group$G$. [r]{a)} For which$v$do all finitely generated soluble groups belong to the class${ K}_v$? [r]{b)} Does the word$v(x,y)=x^{-1}y^{-1}xy$satisfy this condition?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
